weather_report:
A clear, uncomplicated morning has settled over Subiaco, the kind of blue that feels freshly polished and a touch dazzling. Forecasts hold the sky cloudless into early afternoon, so sunshine will be steady and assertive, with that crisp autumn light that sharpens edges and throws bright, short shadows. It looks inviting rather than harsh right now, but the midday sun will carry a punch and the UV will run high despite the gentle season, so shade will be your friend. Air seems dry and visibility clean, the sort of clarity that makes colours ring true and slows the mind to a quieter pace.

Dress simply: breathable layers, sunglasses, a brimmed hat, sunscreen on the collarbones and forearms; carry some water if you’re out and about. It’s a good window for ordinary pleasures—a stroll to pick up bread or a book, a coffee taken on a shaded veranda, laundry on the line, a tidy of the garden while the light is kind. If you plan a longer wander, aim for the later afternoon when the glare softens and the streets feel unhurried again.
